title: Replace N+1 Queries with Single JOIN Query
description: Instead of fetching data in a loop (N+1 pattern), use a single query with JOINs to fetch all data at once

after.code:
# OPTIMIZED: Single Query with JOIN
def get_items_for_reorder() -> dict[str, list]:
    reorder_table = frappe.qb.DocType("Item Reorder")
    item_table = frappe.qb.DocType("Item")
    
    # FIX: One query fetches ALL data via JOIN
    query = (
        frappe.qb.from_(reorder_table)
        .inner_join(item_table)
        .on(reorder_table.parent == item_table.name)
        .select(
            # Reorder fields
            reorder_table.warehouse,
            reorder_table.warehouse_reorder_level,
            reorder_table.warehouse_reorder_qty,
            # Item fields (joined)
            item_table.name,
            item_table.stock_uom,
            item_table.description,
            item_table.item_name,
            item_table.brand,
        )
        .where(
            (item_table.disabled == 0)
            & (item_table.is_stock_item == 1)
        )
    )
    
    data = query.run(as_dict=True)
    
    # Single iteration over results
    itemwise_reorder = {}
    for d in data:
        itemwise_reorder.setdefault(d.name, []).append(d)
    
    return itemwise_reorder
# Total queries: 1 (regardless of item count)

energy_impact: Reduces database round-trips from O(N) to O(1). For 100 items: 201 queries → 1 query. Massive reduction in network overhead and database load.
key_insight: Use Query Builder with JOINs to fetch related data in a single query instead of looping with individual queries.
